category_title: Children at risk of emotional and behavioral disorders (EBD) exhibiting maladaptive behaviors
minimal_context: The student is engaging in a play-based learning activity in the classroom and is asked to transition to the next routine.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to finish your play-based learning activity and transition to the next routine.

STUDENT: (Turns away from the teacher, showing a refusal to participate and difficulty with the transition.)

TUTOR: Please put the toys down and come over here.

STUDENT: No! (Displays persistent defiance and withdraws further away from the peers in the classroom.)

TUTOR: We need to move on to the next activity now.

STUDENT: (Throws the toys across the room, exhibiting an excessive tantrum and an aggressive response to the frustration of the transition.)

options.A: Moving the student to sit at the front desks in the classroom
options.B: Avoiding structured routines and instead allowing the student to self-select play-based materials that interest them
options.C: Creating a nurturing, structured, and responsive environment through interactions and the physical setting
options.D: Recording the behavioral data in scatter plots and ABC charts when school finishes
